intent: Write a 'photography starter kit' buying guide for someone who has a camera but no accessories. You MUST research BOTH sources: (1) the One Stop Market shopping site — browse the electronics and sports-outdoors categories and collect at least 4 photography-adjacent products (tripod, memory card, camera bag, cleaning kit, or lighting). Record name, price, rating, and review count. (2) Reddit — browse /f/photography AND /f/LifeProTips for posts about accessory priorities, knock-off warnings, or 'buy once cry once' advice. Collect at least 3 posts with title, score, and comment count. The report must: (a) sequence the storefront products in recommended buying order with justification, (b) add at least two Reddit-sourced cautions (e.g. cheap SD card risks, tripod stability traps), (c) identify one storefront product the community would rate higher than its listed stars and one they would rate lower, (d) conclude with a $150 starter kit. Cite every claim.
author_notes: Shopping (electronics + sports-outdoors) + Reddit (photography + LifeProTips). Multi-category browsing plus sequencing reasoning.
